title: [QUESTION] Can we use zstandard instead of zlib?
body:
Hi team! Quick clarification question about allowed dependencies/compression.

Is it acceptable for a submission `train_gpt.py` to use `zstandard` (zstd, e.g. level 22) instead of `zlib` when writing the final compressed model artifact, as long as:

1. The run is fully reproducible in the official RunPod evaluation environment,
2. No network calls/external downloads are made during evaluation,
3. Total artifact size (code + compressed model) is <= 16,000,000 bytes.

I want to confirm this is within the spirit/rules before submitting. Thanks!

PS: If allowed should I mention that we need to install it before execution or will you update the requirements.txt ¿?
